data.body:
GAP_ANALYSIS AC_AMENDMENT (2 findings):

FINDING 1 - UNLINK scan must include deleted ticket's own outbound LINKs:
Add to T4 AC: the UNLINK scan must also scan the deleted ticket's own directory for outbound LINK events (where it is the source). For each such active outbound LINK, write an UNLINK event in the deleted ticket's directory AND (if the relation is reciprocal, e.g. relates_to) emit a reciprocal UNLINK in the partner's directory. This prevents surviving partners from observing stale active relations back to the deleted ticket.

FINDING 2 - Partial-failure ordering and idempotency:
Add to T4 AC: (a) Write UNLINK events FIRST (for all active inbound LINKs pointing to deleted ticket AND outbound LINKs from deleted ticket), THEN write ARCHIVED event, THEN .tombstone.json + .archived marker. (b) If re-invoked against an already-tombstoned ticket, skip gate/children-guard and complete any missing UNLINKs rather than erroring. This ensures partial-failure recovery converges correctly.
